definition: Top-10 S&P 500 weight at 35-38% (vs 27% at 2000 peak) creates mechanical amplification risk through coupling of passive (~55% AUM), momentum (70%+ overlap with AI names), and systematic strategies, with PC1 explaining 45-50% of return variance vs 30-35% historical.
evidence: Top-10 S&P 500 weight ~35-38% vs 27% at 2000 peak
Passive vehicles ~55% of AUM
Momentum factor 70%+ overlap with AI names
PC1 explains 45-50% of return variance vs 30-35% historical
Momentum ENB collapsed to ~25-35, below 2007 quant quake (~30-40) and March 2020 (~40-50)
Momentum-quality inter-factor correlation tripled from +0.15-0.25 to +0.50-0.60
open_questions: Whether $40-60B/month international capital flows create a non-linearity mechanism absent in prior concentration episodes
What would trigger rebalancing cascades vs gradual reversion
Whether 3 years of post-ChatGPT data is sufficient for definitive factor attribution

definition: Momentum factor effective number of bets (Meucci entropy-based metric) has collapsed to ~25-35, below both the 2007 quant quake (~30-40) and March 2020 (~40-50), indicating risk models underestimate momentum exposure by 3-4x.
evidence: ENB ~25-35 current vs ~30-40 in 2007 and ~40-50 in 2020
Risk models underestimate momentum exposure by 3-4x
Multi-factor portfolios holding both momentum and quality run ~2x intended AI exposure
open_questions: At what ENB level does forced rebalancing cascade begin
Whether risk model recalibration by large allocators would itself trigger the unwind

definition: Momentum-quality inter-factor correlation has tripled from +0.15-0.25 to +0.50-0.60, meaning multi-factor portfolios holding both are running approximately 2x intended AI exposure with no diversification benefit between these factors.
evidence: Momentum-quality correlation tripled from +0.15-0.25 to +0.50-0.60
Multi-factor portfolios running ~2x intended AI exposure
Diversification benefit between momentum and quality factors has collapsed
open_questions: Whether factor decorrelation could occur gradually or requires a shock
Impact on risk parity and factor-neutral strategies at scale

definition: Hyperscaler OCF/capex ratio of 1.5-1.7x eliminates the debt-driven bust mechanism seen in prior tech cycles, converting potential crash risk to slow-grind duration risk over 3-7+ years. Historical analogues (Japan 1990s-2000s, energy majors 2010-2014) both produced slow grinds because no creditor-imposed stop-loss existed.
evidence: OCF/capex ratio 1.5-1.7x for hyperscalers
Japanese corporates 1990s-2000s: TOPIX underperformed MSCI World by ~60% over 13 years
Energy majors 2010-2014: P/E compressed from 14x to 8x over 2 years
Hyperscaler CDS tight: MSFT 5Y ~30bp, AMZN ~40bp
No creditor-imposed stop-loss in either historical analogue
open_questions: Whether $40-60B/month international capital flows create a non-linearity absent in Japan/energy analogues
Whether US shareholder activism provides capex discipline mechanism altering the slow-grind pattern
N=2 for self-funded bust analogues is thin

definition: Stock-based compensation creates ~$75-80B annual Mag-7 earnings quality distortion through an SBC-restructuring-buyback triangle that is multiplicative, making true FCF-to-equity approximately 60-70% of reported non-GAAP FCF, with reverse reflexive amplification potential of 1.5-2.0x on drawdowns.
evidence: Mag-7 SBC ~$75-80B annually
True FCF-to-equity ~60-70% of reported non-GAAP FCF
25% Mag-7 decline mechanically increases SBC cost by $15-25B, compressing non-GAAP margins 100-200bp
Meta 2022 partial validation: SBC/revenue rose from ~18% to 25%+
Quality factor contamination: 100-200bp (30-40% of measured QMJ premium) is SBC artifact
open_questions: Whether SBC expense acceleration during drawdowns could trigger a reflexive spiral
Degree to which investors have already adjusted for SBC distortion

definition: In equity drawdowns, SBC expense mechanically increases (more shares needed for same dollar compensation), creating a reverse reflexive loop: a 25% Mag-7 decline increases SBC cost by $15-25B and compresses non-GAAP margins 100-200bp, amplifying the drawdown by 1.5-2.0x.
evidence: 25% decline produces $15-25B additional SBC cost
100-200bp non-GAAP margin compression from reflexive SBC increase
Meta 2022 precedent: SBC/revenue rose from ~18% to 25%+
Amplification factor estimated at 1.5-2.0x
open_questions: Whether companies would restructure compensation to break the loop
Interaction with share buyback programs during drawdowns

definition: Server useful-life extensions (Microsoft from 4 to 6 years, adding $3.7B annually to operating income) are a one-directional earnings management ratchet. Further extensions face physical obsolescence constraints (GPU cycles 18-30 months) and auditor pushback. The depreciation cliff when current lives expire is mechanically certain. Aggregate Mag-7 impact: ~$8-12B annually.
evidence: Microsoft 10-K: server life extended 4 to 6 years, $3.7B annual operating income impact (~4% of total)
Google: ~$2.8B estimated impact
Aggregate Mag-7: ~$8-12B annually
GPU generation cycles of 18-30 months constrain further extensions
One-directional ratchet: no further extension possible beyond 6-7 years
open_questions: Exact timing of depreciation cliff for each hyperscaler
Whether new accounting interpretations could further extend useful lives

definition: The ratio of AI-related capital expenditure to attributable AI revenue remains elevated at ~2.5-4.5x after debate adjustment (KB estimate 4-6x), representing the central vulnerability metric for AI equity valuations. Both numerator uncertainty (what counts as AI capex) and denominator uncertainty (what counts as AI revenue) persist.
evidence: KB original estimate: 4-6x at confidence 8
Debate-adjusted range: ~2.5-4.5x after stress-testing from both sides
Challenger_01's 1.5-3.0x refuted (defensive capex inclusion = definitional gerrymandering)
Export controls permanently shrink achievable TAM by 15-25%
Ratio must compress for both equity valuations and dollar capital flow support to sustain
open_questions: Whether AI revenue definition can ever be standardized across companies
Timeline for capex/revenue ratio to converge to sustainable levels
Whether defensive capex should be partially attributed to AI

definition: The 1990s demonstrate that equity returns and productivity realization are independent during the investment phase of a GPT cycle: NASDAQ declined 78% in 2000-2002 while productivity grew 4.1% in 2002. Value accrued to infrastructure users (retailers, banks), not builders (WorldCom, Nortel). Current AI prices conflate 'AI productivity is real' with 'AI investors capture the value.'
evidence: NASDAQ -78% coincident with 4.1% productivity growth in 2002
WorldCom and Nortel bankrupt by 2002 despite building productivity-enabling infrastructure
Value captured by users of infrastructure, not builders
GPT value capture base rate: 1/6 episodes where investors captured value
open_questions: Whether hyperscaler vertical integration (cloud + data + compute + distribution) breaks the historical pattern
Whether the builder/user distinction applies differently to cloud platforms that are both

definition: A four-stage cross-asset pattern (breadth deterioration, secondary beneficiary peaking, curve signal, credit widening) where equity leads credit by 6-12 months with 83% historical hit rate. Currently active in AI-disrupted sectors: legacy media equity down 25-30% while IG spreads at 120-180bp vs historical distressed 300-500bp.
evidence: Pattern appeared in 3/3 well-documented episodes (1998-2002, 2005-2008, 2012-2016)
5/6 episodes where negative equity-credit basis exceeded 2-sigma, equity was correct forward signal
Legacy media equity down 25-30% from peaks while IG spreads remain 120-180bp
Breadth deterioration signal (equal-weighted underperformance 800-1200bp) already active, matching 1998 timing
Credit widening should accelerate by H2 2026-H1 2027 if historical 6-12 month lead holds
open_questions: Whether current divergence will follow historical sequencing or resolve differently
Which specific AI-disrupted sectors show the widest equity-credit basis
Whether passive credit indexation distorts the Stage 4 signal

definition: Value-growth spread at 5th-8th percentile post-1963 implies 400-700bp annualized value factor returns over 5-7 years, with no negative 5-year outcome in sample. Uniquely asymmetric: cheap quintile is non-distressed (financials, utilities, energy at 8-12x with stable cash flows) while expensive quintile is entirely AI narrative-driven.
evidence: Value spread at 5th-8th percentile post-1963
No negative 5-year outcome in historical sample at these levels
Cheap quintile: financials, utilities, energy at 8-12x with stable cash flows
Expensive quintile entirely AI narrative-driven
Historical 400-700bp annualized returns over 5-7 years from similar levels
open_questions: Timing unreliable: 1999-2000 spread widened further 12-18 months before reverting violently
Whether AI narrative moderation alone is sufficient catalyst or macro shock needed

definition: US semiconductor export controls operate as an institutional ratchet with 60-70% probability of further tightening vs <10% rollback. Three rounds of escalation (Oct 2022, Oct 2023, Jan 2025) demonstrate asymmetric institutional dynamics where tightening is costless politically while loosening requires multiple veto points. Bipartisan consensus makes de-escalation <15% regardless of election outcome.
evidence: Three rounds of escalation: Oct 2022, Oct 2023, Jan 2025
NSC, BIS, and congressional committees all favor tightening; no actor benefits from loosening
NVIDIA China revenue declined from ~25% to ~12-15%
Compound supply chain tax: 15-25% tariff + 5-10% rerouting + 3-5% compliance = 23-40% effective cost
China hawk is zero-cost politically; China dove is career-ending
open_questions: Whether further tightening targets foundry access (TSMC) or specific chip architectures
Impact of enforcement gaps and third-country circumvention on effective control

definition: TSMC's ~90% share of advanced node (<5nm) production is underpriced systemic geopolitical risk qualitatively different from standard tail risks due to non-substitutability. No semiconductor strategic reserve exists (2-8 week inventories). A Taiwan contingency simultaneously disrupts physical supply, triggers $300-500B+ forced divestment, and freezes AI capex for 12-24+ months.
evidence: ~90% of sub-5nm production concentrated in Taiwan
Finished chip inventories: 2-8 weeks with no strategic reserve
CHIPS Act diversification timeline: 8-12 years for meaningful non-Taiwan capacity
Taiwan CDS at 30-40bp implies ~2-3% probability vs analyst assessments of 5-8% cumulative over 5 years
Conjunction of supply disruption + forced divestment + capex freeze uncaptured by any single hedge
open_questions: Whether accelerated CHIPS Act spending could meaningfully reduce the 8-12 year diversification timeline
How to hedge a risk that no single instrument captures
Whether Samsung/Intel backfill could partially substitute at lower nodes

definition: The divergence between equity valuations (Mag-7 forward P/E 35-40x non-GAAP), rates (10Y TIPS 2.0-2.2% consistent with 1.5-1.8% trend GDP), and credit (tight spreads) has widened. Duration math confirms rates ARE relevant: 50bp real rate move produces 12-15% Mag-7 fair value change. At 35-40x forward, Mag-7 effective equity duration is ~25-30 years.
evidence: Mag-7 forward P/E 35-40x (non-GAAP)
10Y TIPS at 2.0-2.2% consistent with 1.5-1.8% trend GDP growth
Capex-to-revenue ratio has NOT improved despite continued 30-50% capex growth
50bp real rate move = 12-15% Mag-7 fair value change
Mag-7 effective equity duration ~25-30 years at current multiples
open_questions: Fraction attributable to equilibrium winner-take-all vs mispricing remains undetermined
Whether capex/revenue improving 3+ quarters would resolve the inconsistency

definition: The mid-1990s internet cycle is the primary historical analogue for the current AI cycle, with structural criteria matching (incumbent-led, GPT, tight labor market). Current cycle maps to ~1996-1998 (mid-cycle expansion), with 1-3 years of expansion runway under the modal outcome. Base rate: 0/5 analogues produced bust directly from mid-cycle without identifiable late-cycle signals.
evidence: Structural criteria match: incumbent-led, GPT, tight labor market
0/5 analogues produced bust from mid-cycle without late-cycle signals
CMA factor sign flip ~3 years in matching 1995-2000 duration of 5 years
Ordinal ranking survives: internet mid-1990s best analogue, telecom second
open_questions: Timing uncertainty is wide at plus/minus 1-3 years per phase
Whether self-funded nature makes this cycle systematically different from 1990s
Cardinal similarity scores refuted as methodological theater

definition: The Kalecki profit identity links AI capex (~$250B) to aggregate S&P 500 earnings as the marginal growth driver, though the fiscal deficit ($1.8-2T) is 10-15x larger and provides a floor. A 25% capex cut (~$60-75B) is only 3-4% of the fiscal deficit floor. The directional channel is real but magnitude is more modest than initially framed.
evidence: AI capex ~$250B sustains growth at the margin
Fiscal deficit floor ~$1.8-2T provides insulation
25% AI capex cut (~$60-75B) is 3-4% of fiscal deficit floor
Accounting identity is irrefutable; magnitude was moderated in debate
open_questions: Whether fiscal floor erodes if consolidation occurs simultaneously
Second-order effects through employment and supplier networks

definition: The US dollar is in a capex-sustained plateau with an AI capital flow premium estimated at 5-8 cents EUR/USD (wide confidence intervals: 3-12 cents). DXY at 103-107 despite 100-150bp Fed cuts confirms rate differentials are not the primary driver. TIC data shows $40-60B/month net equity inflows concentrated in tech.
evidence: DXY 103-107 despite 100-150bp Fed cuts
TIC data: $40-60B/month net equity inflows concentrated in tech
ECB-Fed differential narrowed ~100bp+ with minimal EUR/USD response
Historical parallel: 1997-2001 DXY +25% without systematic rate advantage
AI premium is structural (3-5 year), not cyclical
open_questions: The 5-8 cent premium estimate could range from 3-12 cents
Whether capital flow reversal would be gradual or discontinuous

definition: Treasury basis trade at $800B-$1T at 50-100x leverage creates the most important systemic amplification channel through cross-margining with AI equity positions at multi-strategy hedge funds. This is the primary mechanism by which an AI equity event could become a rates crisis. Dealer inventory capacity at $30-40B vs $250B pre-GFC.
evidence: Basis trade $800B-$1T at 50-100x leverage
Cross-margining links AI equity and Treasury positions at multi-strategy funds
March 2020 validated channel at approximately half current scale
Dealer inventory capacity $30-40B vs $250B pre-GFC
SEC central clearing mandate Q2 2026 creates calendar-identifiable catalyst
open_questions: Whether current scale vs 2020 creates qualitatively different dynamics
Impact of SEC central clearing mandate on basis trade economics
Whether Fed would intervene proactively given known vulnerability

definition: Three mechanically certain margin headwinds converge 2027-2029 for hyperscalers: depreciation cliff ($70-120B delta = 300-500bp), useful-life benefit exhaustion ($8-12B fully captured), and rising energy costs (data center electricity 4-5% to 8-12% of US generation). Combined: 400-700bp operating margin pressure.
evidence: Depreciation: $200-250B annual capex at 5yr/6yr life = ~$170-210B D&A by 2028 vs ~$80-100B current
Delta: $70-120B = 300-500bp margin pressure
Useful-life benefit: $8-12B fully captured, no further extensions possible
Energy: PJM capacity 10x, data center electricity rising from 4-5% to 8-12% of US generation
open_questions: Whether AI revenue growth could offset margin compression in the denominator
Exact timing of convergence depends on capex trajectory 2025-2027

definition: Across six historical General Purpose Technology episodes, infrastructure builders captured primary value in only 1/6 (17% naive). Adjusted to 25-40% for hyperscaler vertical integration (cloud + data + compute + distribution). Market-implied probability of 70-80% represents a conjunction fallacy dramatically exceeding the adjusted base rate.
evidence: 1/6 GPT episodes where infrastructure builders captured value (17% naive)
Differentiating variable is bottleneck control
Hyperscalers control distribution, data, compute, and customer relationships — unprecedented
Adjusted base rate 25-40% vs market-implied 70-80%
open_questions: Whether vertical integration genuinely raises the base rate or just delays value leakage
Which specific bottleneck controls are most durable over time

definition: ROIC decomposition by capital vintage shows inexorable blended ROIC decline toward WACC. Pre-2022 legacy capital (~$600-800B) earns 25-35% ROIC; 2022-2024 AI-era capital (~$350-500B) earns an estimated 5-10%; 2025-2027 incremental (~$400-600B) assumed 3-8%. At 30-50% capex growth, blended ROIC reaches ~12-15% by 2028, approaching 9-11% WACC.
evidence: Pre-2022 capital: legacy ROIC 25-35%
2022-2024 AI-era capital: estimated ROIC 5-10%
2025-2027 incremental: assumed 3-8%
At zero capex growth blended declines from ~22-25% to ~14-17% by 2028
At 30-50% growth blended reaches ~12-15%, approaching 9-11% WACC
open_questions: 2022-2024 vintage ROIC has plus/minus 5pp uncertainty due to revenue attribution opacity
Whether newer GPU architectures improve capital efficiency of later vintages

definition: Stock-bond correlation is currently positive (+0.1 to +0.3 average) driven by three forces: AI capex demand (inflationary), no productivity supply yet (no TFP break), and fiscal dominance (inflationary). Risk parity portfolios calibrated to 2010-2020 negative correlation regime are running ~20-30% more equity-equivalent risk than intended.
evidence: Rolling 6-month stock-bond correlation: +0.15 to +0.35 since mid-2024
Three-force decomposition: AI capex demand + no TFP break + fiscal dominance
Risk parity portfolios running ~20-30% excess equity-equivalent risk
Regime is stage-dependent (could shift with AI productivity supply), not permanent
open_questions: Whether AI productivity breakthrough would flip correlation back to negative
Timeline for portfolio reallocation by institutional investors

definition: Real rates at ~2.0-2.2% 10Y TIPS are the cross-asset fulcrum with asymmetric impacts. 50bp rise: Mag-7 -12-15%, S&P 500 -6-8%, IG spreads +10-20bp, DXY +2-3%. 50bp decline helps equities but weakens dollar, stressing the capex-profit-inflow feedback loop. Creates a 'no good outcome' dynamic for US-centric multi-asset portfolios.
evidence: 50bp real rate rise: Mag-7 -12-15%, S&P 500 -6-8%, IG spreads +10-20bp, DXY +2-3%
50bp real rate decline helps equities but weakens dollar
Asymmetry creates no-good-outcome for US multi-asset portfolios
Sensitivity estimates subject to plus/minus 30% estimation error
open_questions: Whether the no-good-outcome dynamic holds across all rate move magnitudes
Interaction with Fed policy path and forward guidance

definition: Global sovereign AI industrial policy ($600B+) creates fiscal path dependency constraining consolidation by 0.3-0.5% GDP in the US. Once a $20B fab is under construction, political cost of failure exceeds economic cost of continuation (F-35 analogy: distributed across 45 states to prevent cancellation).
evidence: US CHIPS Act: $52B + $24B tax credits
EU Chips Act: EUR43B; Japan ~$25-65B; South Korea ~$450B including private
F-35 analogy: distributed across 45 states for political lock-in
Sunk-cost political economy makes expenditures sticky regardless of ROI
open_questions: Whether fiscal consolidation pressure could override political path dependency
Impact on sovereign credit ratings and term premia

definition: The Fed 'AI put' is structurally impaired relative to 1998-2001 precedent. Current inflation at 2.5-3.5% limits easing to 150-250bp within 6 months (vs 550bp in 2001). The Fed faces a trilemma: intervene in Treasuries (fiscal dominance), cut rates (unanchor inflation), or stand pat (deleveraging cascade). The basis trade could impair Treasury transmission.
evidence: Current inflation 2.5-3.5% vs less than 2% in 2000-2001
Easing capacity: 150-250bp in 6 months vs 550bp in 2001
Average easing/tightening asymmetry ratio: 3.5x across 3 episodes
2020 precedent misleading: Fed printed into deflationary shock; AI bust with 3%+ inflation has no analogue
open_questions: Whether inflation would decelerate enough during an AI bust to widen easing capacity
How basis trade coupling would interact with Fed Treasury operations

definition: The CMA (conservative-minus-aggressive) factor is ~3 years into a potential 5-7 year sign flip cycle where aggressive investment outperforms conservative. Self-funded booms persist longer. Historical mean reversion produces 3-5 sigma losses within 12 months. Monitoring metric: capex/OCF crossing 55-65% for 2-3 hyperscalers (projected late 2027).
evidence: CMA returned -200 to -400bp in 1994-2000, -150 to -300bp in 2010-2014, -200 to -350bp currently
2000-2001 reversal: +25% cumulative CMA return within 18 months
Self-funded booms persist longer due to no financing constraint
Small-n problem: 2-3 episodes
open_questions: Whether capex/OCF threshold is the right trigger indicator
Small-n problem limits precision on timing and magnitude

definition: Three incompatible AI regulatory regimes (EU AI Act, US EO, China regulations) create de facto market segmentation with $2-8B annual compliance costs across top 20 firms. EU-compliant AI models may be structurally less capable due to data restrictions, creating a regulatory capability gap and a structural EUR-negative factor independent of monetary policy.
evidence: Three incompatible regulatory regimes
$2-8B annual compliance costs across top 20 firms
EU-compliant models face data restrictions and transparency requirements
Brussels Effect applies but creates capability gap
open_questions: Whether regulatory convergence is possible on any meaningful timeline
Whether the capability gap widens or narrows over time

definition: TWD and KRW have functionally reclassified from EM currencies to semiconductor proxy status. TWD/KRW-SOX correlation has risen from ~0.3 to ~0.7 while R-squared against traditional EM factors (VIX, EMBI, DXY) has declined. EM FX strategies including TWD/KRW in standard baskets are systematically mispricing factor exposure.
evidence: TWD/KRW-SOX correlation risen from ~0.3 to ~0.7
R-squared against traditional EM factors declining
Taiwan CA surplus ~14% GDP from TSMC
Geopolitical tail risk qualitatively different from standard EM political risk
open_questions: How to properly classify these currencies in portfolio construction
Whether other EM currencies will similarly reclassify around specific industries

definition: The AI-driven nuclear renaissance faces a uranium/HALEU supply-chain geopolitical bottleneck. Kazakhstan (43% global uranium, Russian sphere) and Russia (35-40% global enrichment) dominate supply. US imports ~90% enriched uranium. Centrus produces ~900 kg HALEU/year vs 40+ tonnes commercial need by 2030.
evidence: Kazakhstan: 43% global uranium production in Russian sphere of influence
Russia: 35-40% global enrichment capacity
US imports ~90% of enriched uranium
Centrus: ~900 kg HALEU/year vs 40+ tonnes needed by 2030
5-10 GW AI-driven nuclear capacity announced
open_questions: Timeline for US domestic enrichment capacity buildout
Whether SMR deployment timelines are realistic given HALEU constraints

definition: Stock-based compensation contaminates the quality (QMJ) factor: 100-200bp of the measured quality premium (30-40% of total) is an SBC artifact. When sorted on GAAP rather than non-GAAP earnings, 2-3 Mag-7 names shift from the top quintile to the 2nd-3rd quintile.
evidence: 100-200bp of QMJ premium is SBC artifact (30-40% of measured premium)
2-3 Mag-7 names shift from top to 2nd-3rd quintile on GAAP sort
Quality factor contamination confirmed across independent analytical frameworks
open_questions: Whether factor providers will recalibrate quality definitions to account for SBC
Impact on smart beta products tracking quality factors

definition: PC1 explains 45-50% of S&P 500 return variance (vs 30-35% historical), contaminating market-neutral portfolios with ~15-25% unhedged AI directional exposure. Evidence from 2022: 3-8% unexplained drawdowns in nominally market-neutral strategies.
evidence: PC1 explains 45-50% of variance vs 30-35% historical
Market-neutral portfolios carry ~15-25% unhedged AI directional exposure
2022: 3-8% unexplained drawdowns in market-neutral strategies
open_questions: Whether explicit AI factor hedging is feasible and cost-effective
How risk models should incorporate PC1 contamination

definition: Top-of-book depth in Mag-7 has declined ~40-60% since 2019 despite rising volume, creating an appearance of liquidity that evaporates under stress. Combined with 0DTE options creating concentrated negative gamma (daily notional >$1T), market-makers must sell into declining prices during stress.
evidence: Top-of-book depth in Mag-7 declined ~40-60% since 2019
Volume rising despite depth declining
0DTE daily notional >$1T creating concentrated negative gamma
Market-makers hedging short gamma must sell into declines
open_questions: Whether 0DTE gamma effects are large enough to trigger cross-asset cascades
Interaction between declining depth and passive rebalancing flows

definition: Semiconductor and AI hardware supply chains face a compound effective cost increase of 23-40% from the combination of tariffs (15-25%), rerouting costs (5-10%), and compliance overhead (3-5%), calculated from statutory inputs. This compounds with export control restrictions.
evidence: 15-25% tariff component
5-10% rerouting cost component
3-5% compliance cost component
23-40% total effective cost from statutory inputs
open_questions: Whether supply chain reorganization could reduce the rerouting component over time
